accept: Either: every command accepting an id resolves true unique-prefix lookup (e.g. act-c2 if no other id starts with c2), OR every doc/help string is updated to drop 'prefix ok' and require the full short_id.

Error path distinguishes 'not_found' (no issue with that prefix) from 'id_ambiguous' (prefix matches multiple); both surface in the {error, message, details} envelope with the candidate list when ambiguous.
Tests cover: full short id, full canonical id, ambiguous prefix, non-matching prefix, exact-id-of-tombstoned issue.
payload.description:
Discovered 2026-05-10 by UX-eval agent. Flag help and the spec consistently say 'prefix ok' for ids (e.g. 'parent issue id (full or unique prefix)', '--under <id> (prefix ok)'). Reality:

  act show 0d9d  -> works (full short id)
  act show act-0d9d  -> works (full canonical id)
  act show 0d  -> 'no issue matches "0d"' (3-char prefix rejected)
  act show act-0  -> 'no issue matches "act-0"' (1-hex-char prefix rejected)

So the ONLY accepted forms are the full short_id and the full canonical id. Every doc that says 'prefix ok' is wrong. Compounding: 'no issue matches' is the same error for 'no such id' AND 'ambiguous prefix matches N issues' — agents have no way to distinguish.

Either implement true unique-prefix lookup (the natural reading of the docs and the obvious agent ergonomic) or strip 'prefix ok' / 'unique prefix' from every help string. internal/ids/prefix.go appears to compute shortest-unique prefixes already — possible the lookup just isn't using it.
payload.title: ID prefix matching contradicts docs (only full short id works)
